steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of a specific dinosaur fossil specimen's nickname — not just state that nicknames exist, but name one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Nicknames are given to many dinosaur fossil specimens, including one housed at a museum in Malta, Montana.': mentions a specimen but never gives its nickname. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at museums all over the world, and many of these specimens are given nicknames.': general statement only; no specific specimen or nickname. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Roberta is the nickname of a Brachylophosaurus fossil specimen housed at the Great Plains Dinosaur Museum and Field Station in Malta, Montana.': names the nickname “Roberta” and the species it belongs to — a direct example. ✓
  • Choice D — 'A Brachylophosaurus fossil specimen from the Late Cretaceous period, which ended more than 65 million years ago, is housed at the Great Plains Dinosaur Museum and Field Station.': describes the specimen without mentioning any nickname. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
